reviews.review_description: It is so beautiful, colorful, interactive and peaceful. It’s short, but you can just keep playing as much as you like, touching flowers as you walk, solving very simple puzzles, and petting the cutest little floating creature. You are bringing light to a dark forest, and you can collect gems to buy flower pots and seeds to plant in those pots. It’s so confusing in the beginning, but it doesn’t take long to figure things out. Just an amazing game!

I enjoyed this game it is very eye pleasing ! Super cute graphics. 

A few comments 
- I finished this game in like two hours. 
- There is no settings, I get that this games vision is for you to just play and interact and figure it out, but I couldn't make my self taller as I had to jump to be able to reach the flowers etc. (I had changed my view in meta settings but still didn't work ) 
- The music is calming and nice, but there was this one place where it sounded like the squid game intro music lol! 
- I was pleased to see there was an option to change to locomotion.
- It would be cool if after you have planeted a flower in the ground you can move it after.. 
- The game doesn't save?  I went back to home to empty my inventory and plant more seeds, and when I went back to the island it all reset?. I get like the little glowing balls are reset as you can get them to buy seeds, but the other missions within the game that allow you to keep going, they reset so I had to complete them all while I was on the island, but annoying as your inventory fills up fast.. 
- Was nice, but would like more islands?

This is the game I will now hold as my ideal, and compare all others to its standard... The graphics are gorgeous (quite a few "Oh WOW" moments during play). It has great music (that actually makes sense in context).  The mechanics feel easy - no frustrations. And I freakin' love the style of play.
I finally found exactly what kind of game play I like best thanks to Fujii... I didn't know it until Fujii showed me. I love the wandering around finding loot, taking my time uncovering new aspects with no pressure! This game is such a joy - I'm glad it is in the world. A big heartfelt thank you to the developers!!!
It is hard to find words to describe the awesomeness of this game. The graphics are beautiful, the music is enchanting and it is so much fun to play. I'll be looking for more stuff by this developer.
It’s difficult to get very far when you have no idea how to play.  Beautiful graphics.

Magical VR experience, explore a large dream like land, interact with the reactive environments, collect seeds, garden at your home. I wish it had a meta quest 3 graphical enhancement update. It is still a very beautiful game. The music and sound effects really add to the ambiance and vibe.
With no way to simple access settings... its a fail... cannot adjust to my preference on movement and snap turning s teleport is a COMPLETE TURN OFF.  Refunded.
So beautiful and relaxing. I love this.

I would give it 1000000 out of 10 it’s very relaxing and music graphic are awesome

Bought it a few years ago when I replay it is still fun 

Recommend it for someone who love music and nature also interaction games

Yesterday I got through what I believe was the first chapter of the game. I decided that I done enough, so I quit the game fully expecting it to save and not have to worry about it. Today I go open the game thinking that if I don’t go in soon. My plants are dying. I go in. It says continue. I go to continue and behold, the game is starting all over again. There’s no indication that I had to click something or do anything in order for the game to continue so can someone please explain to me what I did to make the game not save anymore?

I really love this game! It’s fun just wandering around and seeing everything. There is really no explanation on how to do anything but I kind of liked having to figure it all out on my own. I love being able to plant and take care of a garden and I can lose time when I’m in the game. When you start, you begin at a tree that has continue on one side and new game on the other. When I felt I had been every where there was to go, I decided to do a new game rather than continue the old. That is where the fruit by the tree comes in, you pick one and plant it in the new game pot and start a “new game”. Which actually means you start over again from the beginning, or at least the fruit I picked did that. I’m fine with that because there were things I wished I could change in my garden and now I can. It’s definitely worth the price to get this game.
This is such an incredible game! I am new to VR and this is my first game. I am so impressed with the graphics and the creativity. I could stay in there all day.

Takes just a little bit to figure out the mechanics but then it’s totally Zen! Great visual world and music. The cute little guide is a bonus. Follow him (and maybe give him a tickle) if you get stuck… Enjoy!

10/10. It was magical. Everything was so well thought out. It felt like a cartoon version of the movie Avatar, with these alien plants and animals that respond to touch. I wanted so much more. I will buy sequels or expansion packs without hesitation. So so good.

After completing the main game, I've been building up my gardens, going in almost every day to water my plants, plant new stuff, move things around... it's been a great way to destress after work. My only complaints are around plants disappearing. In the desert biome, there are a lot of plants that disappear from a distance (but reappear when you get closer). Then today when I logged in, a HUGE number of my plants were just gone from my main garden. Like, maybe half or more. The pots were still there, and the green free-form "pots" were empty but couldn't be moved. Several plants were also missing from my desert biome, but not quite as many as the first garden. I'm super sad about this and mildly reluctant to plant more stuff. Is there like a limit to how many plants you can have in each biome? Or do they die off after a certain amount of time?

Ok..so I am not your avid game player..and this game although at times tricky to figure out once you get the hang of it its a delightful experience. If you are new to the VR experience or not too much into hardcore gaming this is the game for you! I like relaxing and calming experiences more than anything. This game allows u to grow a beautiful garden, collect things, interact with cute little creatures, and gives you small puzzles and quests. I will say this..and maybe it is because it is still pretty new to me, I do catch myself getting lost. Beautiful game though bright and colorful with enchanting music and sound effects.

It's super relaxing, immersive, and mystical.  The only issue I have and maybe this is just me but I feel awkward giving plants a rub down to make them grow. Lol. The only thing missing is the plant screaming"Oh yes!" Before it grows. 🤣 Other than that cute game.

There are parts of this game that I imagine heaven might be like. It’s like floating through an dream surrounded by cute friendly little critters. Even the flowers are friendly. My only complaint is that I wish there were more sections to unlock and explore. Would maybe need an additional garden room if that were the case. Very beautiful game though!
It’s fun, and a much needed break from reality. Mom’s get in here and game with your kids! It’s really the future!

reviews.review_title: The VR experience I’ve been wanting!
reviews.review_description: I love this game. You have a home base, your garden, that you can customize and rearrange as you see fit. Or you can journey out into fantastic landscapes and explore, collecting seeds and other things to bring home with you. There’s even some light puzzle solving! If you’re looking for a very relaxed vibe and want to experience the beauty of VR, you can’t go wrong here. Highly recommended!

This game is a nice little escape for me to feel like Mother Nature for awhile. Your “home” is  colorful biodiverse garden in a large temple-like dome. There are little side-quests when you venture out into the wild via boat with your little companion playing you music to collect seeds and glowing balls which act sort of like currency (buy seeds and pots, open locked doors). Each direction takes you to a different type of environment (forest, sandy beach desert, grassy field,Each full grown plant produces a glowing ball. The game has soothing airy music, and sweet little animals. 

There’s some sort of launching pad outside my night- blooming door that I can’t seem to unlock. But other than that, my curiosity led me to rush through every area. Once you do that, and plant all the flowers, there is not much else to do, so try to take it in a little at a time. 
If you like games with a lot going on, this isn’t for you.

reviews.review_title: A wander land for the whole family
reviews.review_description: Like this game.  Everyone in a family including kids and grandparents can play this game. It's like a wander land for a young kid. Very relaxing and the way to collect seeds for the garden is interesting.
This game is adorable, the puzzles aren’t totally impossible, and it’s entertaining... worth the price!

Hi! I'm having an issue with the game. When my head is too close to a solid object like a stair or wall, I lose my 6 degrees of freedom and the world starts moving around me as if I was only in 3DOF, which is really disorienting. I dont know if this was a purposeful method of keeping people from clipping through floors or just a glitch but I would prefer if it was replaced with just telling me to take a step back or asking me to return to the play space like in shadow point. I'll write a real reveiw once I've actually played more of the game. All I can say right now is that its very pretty!
Update: I've played more of the game and didnt have any more orientation issues. I wanted to update with a more positive reveiw because I'm having a great time after exploring 2 of the 3 areas. They're actually quite big, and there's plenty of secrets and hidden pathways. It feels like that scene in avatar when hes running through the forest for the first time, touching everything and watching it react. Definitely an experience that's only possible in vr
How can I kill grass?
Very kind and meditative exploration game

This is a lovely game if you want a break from zombie slaying or waving sabers about! 

Explore beautiful environments and solve some simple puzzles to find seeds for your garden. Grow some exotic plants and water them often. 

The more you explore and collect, the more of the quirky and chilled landscape becomes available to you.

The ambient music is lovely and mellow. The gameplay is at your own speed. The graphics are vibrant and colourful. Your home garden is fun to grow in.

Yes, a definite recommendation from me. A bit of a surprise hit!
I wanted a zen like game to relax to before I settle down for  the night  and  it's cute,  fun but best of all so relaxing 😌 LOVE IT!!!!
Most beautiful game on the quest. It is so satisfying, relaxing and very well put together. It’s a masterpiece and my favorite game right now. 100% worth $15.

So i bought this game today and im having a really great time the weird floppy arms are really weird and the artstyle in general is amazing and beautiful. 

I found the gameplay to be really interesting and different to most anything I’ve ever played before. 

Special mention to the inventory system by the way, its really interesting that its a buch of flower heads that act like “pockets” of sorts that you can place all sorts of things in, very cool design here.

The only feature this game needs is an option for smooth turning and smooth movement with the left thumbstick but keep teleportation on the right. Ive always found the teleporting and snapping rotation to be really immersion breaking for me, could we get these features as optional things devs? Pleeeeease =)

Overall though I’m having a blast this is fantastic if you like chill puzzle games

reviews.review_title: What I have been waiting forever
reviews.review_description: What a cool fun environment. Very gentle and interesting experience. One to show your friends for sure. Great work developers

This game offers so much in look, feel, play, and joy. It doesn't assault your senses but uses them with style and creativity. If you enjoy nature adventures, calming environments, original music, whimsy, and beautiful graphics, Fuji has it all. It is thoughtfully and  lovingly designed. It promotes community and interaction with nature. There is humor and fun throughout, and many vistas to enjoy. A major bonus is the home garden area that offers endless possibilities of design enjoyment.  And if your creations get out of hand, the magic trowel let's you remove things (erase actually) and make needed changes. The most value ever in a VR game for me.
This game is everything I've ever wanted in a VR game. Cute creatures, beautiful worlds to explore and thoughtful.

The feeling of wonder I experienced playing this as one of my first VR experiences convinced me 100% that VR is the future of gaming and entertainment as a whole! This game is so beautiful! My only complaint is that there wasn't more.

reviews.review_title: This is what VR was made for
reviews.review_description: Extremely immersive and trippy experience with a beautiful theme. You can play the game while sitting on the floor too.

Second only to beatsaber in VR notoriety, this game is delightful and immersive. I ezpecially appreciate this game after shifting from "VR guest" to "VR regular." The exploration and discovery are co.pelling in their own right, but now I find myself returning to build and maintain a beautiful virtual garden as part of my daily routine.
Beautiful and peaceful game , great for relaxing and exploring

This is such a soothing little adventure. Cute visuals, great music and sound effects, lot’s of wow moments.
For when you want a relaxing vr experience. I do wish it was a bit longer though.

I agree with SlipSlot on ALL the interaction and play...its fun to just explore and plant things to grow. However that writer said he has a bad back and wished for a sitting position. When Oculus starts up and asks to level the floor...you do have an option for stationary position...I like using it ...sit down and press confirm for sitting stationary. Happy playing all ;)

My husband and I PT the Oculus quest a few days ago for Christmas (opened it early of course). Of all the mind blowing games we have purchased, this is my first review. Just wow!!!! Why isn’t this on the top list of suggestions for newbies? Although, this game is not action packed in the slightest, It is magical.  It takes you to another beautiful world and I am thrilled I bought it. 

Ps. If you have a kiddo dying to get his/her hands on your Oculus, but you don’t want him playing a shootem up game, his is a winner.
